title: EnagÃ¡sâ€™ Hydrogen Day Confirms the Take-Off of Green Hydrogen
content: EnagÃ¡sâ€™ Hydrogen Day confirms the take off of green hydrogen by bringing together prominent representatives from the Spanish Government, the European Commission and the German Government, highlighting strong institutional support for renewable hydrogen and the development of a regulated market that reinforces competitiveness and drives demand. Sara Aagesen opened the event and stated that over the coming months a draft bill will be submitted to address the transposition of the European Hydrogen Package to create a domestic hydrogen system, establish a new regulated market, boost demand for hydrogen and renewable gases and provide the tools needed to develop the required infrastructure, while Teresa Ribera reaffirmed the European Commissionâ€™s firm commitment to hydrogen in Europe and emphasised that H2med is one of the most advanced European energy highway projects and remains a priority. Cani FernÃ¡ndez stressed that hydrogen is a joint venture requiring collaboration from governments, regulators, industry and academia across all countries. EnagÃ¡s CEO Arturo Gonzalo highlighted that the administrative, engineering and commercial development of the Spanish Backbone Network and H2med are progressing at cruising speed with confirmed feasibility and commissioning dates. Major industries and mobility sector leaders reaffirmed their commitment to green hydrogen as a key vector for competitive decarbonisation. The 4th Hydrogen Day also underlined the need for a European hydrogen infrastructure network capable of connecting supply and demand at scale, supported by record investment decisions, over 110 billion dollars globally, and increased European funding proposals, demonstrating strong momentum behind hydrogen infrastructure growth
author: Analysts Account
